name: 4 A's Test for Delirium Screening
description: Rapid four-item bedside screening tool for delirium, designed for routine use without special training

en.name: 4 A's Test for Delirium Screening
en.shortDescription: Rapid four-item bedside screening tool for delirium, designed for routine use without special training
en.longDescription: ## Definition & Clinical Context The 4AT (4 'A's Test) is a rapid screening tool for delirium, designed for use in routine clinical practice without special training. It takes less than 2 minutes to administer and can be used with patients who have visual or hearing impairment, or who are too drowsy or agitated to undergo formal cognitive testing [1]. The instrument comprises four items: - alertness - AMT4 (Abbreviated Mental Test-4, a brief orientation screen) - attention (months of the year backwards) - acute change or fluctuating course The total score ranges from 0 to 12. A score of 0 suggests delirium is unlikely, 1–3 indicates possible cognitive impairment warranting further investigation, and ≥ 4 suggests possible delirium. ## Included Concepts This concept set contains the SNOMED Observable Entity concept representing the recorded 4AT score: - `3662221` — 4AT (4 A's Test) score (`1239211000000103`) ## Excluded Concepts The following SNOMED concept is intentionally **not** included, as it represents the assessment tool rather than the recorded score value: - `3662219` — 4AT - 4 A's Test (`1239191000000102`, Staging / Scales) ## References [1] Bellelli G, Morandi A, Davis D, et al. Validation of the 4AT, a new instrument for rapid delirium screening: a study in 234 hospitalised older people. *Age Ageing*. 2014;43(4):496-502. doi:10.1093/ageing/afu021. PMID: 24590568

fr.name: 4 A's Test for Delirium Screening score
fr.shortDescription: Outil de dépistage rapide du delirium à quatre items, conçu pour un usage en routine sans formation spécifique
fr.longDescription: ## Définition et contexte clinique Le 4AT (4 'A's Test) est un outil de dépistage rapide du délirium, conçu pour être utilisé en pratique clinique courante sans formation spécifique. Son administration prend moins de 2 minutes et il peut être utilisé chez les patients présentant une déficience visuelle ou auditive, ou trop somnolents ou agités pour réaliser une évaluation cognitive formelle [1]. L'instrument comprend quatre items : - vigilance - AMT4 (Abbreviated Mental Test-4, un test bref d'orientation) - attention (mois de l'année à l'envers) - changement aigu ou évolution fluctuante Le score total varie de 0 à 12. Un score de 0 suggère que le délirium est peu probable, 1–3 indique un trouble cognitif possible nécessitant des investigations complémentaires, et ≥ 4 suggère un délirium possible. ## Concepts inclus Ce concept set contient le concept SNOMED Observable Entity représentant le score 4AT enregistré : - `3662221` — 4AT (4 A's Test) score (`1239211000000103`) ## Concepts exclus Le concept SNOMED suivant est intentionnellement **non inclus**, car il représente l'outil d'évaluation et non la valeur du score enregistré : - `3662219` — 4AT - 4 A's Test (`1239191000000102`, Staging / Scales) ## Références [1] Bellelli G, Morandi A, Davis D, et al. Validation of the 4AT, a new instrument for rapid delirium screening: a study in 234 hospitalised older people. *Age Ageing*. 2014;43(4):496-502. doi:10.1093/ageing/afu021. PMID: 24590568

createdByDetails.affiliation: Univ Rennes, CHU Rennes, INSERM, LTSI-UMR 1099, Rennes, France
createdByDetails.profession: Medical Informatics and Intensive Care Physician

versions.summary: Replaced SNOMED Staging/Scales concept 3662219 (4AT - 4 A's Test, Standard) with SNOMED Observable Entity 3662221 (4AT (4 A's Test) score, Non-standard). Rationale: the Observable Entity represents the recorded score value rather than the assessment tool, which better matches the intended use of this concept set. Also removed includeDescendants (no longer applicable for the Observable Entity).
